speeches.name: Beth M. Hammack
speeches.role: President, Federal Reserve Bank of Cleveland
speeches.summary: Hammack issued a statement explaining her vote at the April 28-29 FOMC meeting, reflecting a careful and data-dependent policy stance.

speeches.name: Christopher J. Waller
speeches.summary: Waller spoke on Federal Reserve Bank operations, a topic that is typically operational rather than a direct signal about the policy path.

speeches.name: Susan M. Collins
speeches.role: President & CEO, Federal Reserve Bank of Boston
speeches.summary: Collins discussed the U.S. economy as resilient but facing risks and uncertainty, suggesting a balanced but watchful outlook.

macro_fred.narrative: Leaning Neutral: CPI at 4.0%, Fed Funds at 3.63%, unemployment at 4.3%.
